title: Agentic Engineering: A Multi-Agent LLM Framework for Solving Chemical Engineering Tasks with Open-Source Thermodynamic Software
novelty_statement: We present a structured multi-agent LLM framework that couples large language model agents with a rigorous open-source thermodynamic library to solve diverse chemical engineering tasks end-to-end, from natural language problem definition through validated simulation to engineering report generation, demonstrating the approach across eight real-world case studies spanning six engineering disciplines.

research_questions.question: Can a structured multi-agent LLM framework reliably orchestrate thermodynamic process simulations from natural language engineering task descriptions?
research_questions.hypothesis: A framework combining specialized LLM agents with a rigorous thermodynamic library can produce validated engineering results across diverse task types.
research_questions.method: Demonstrate across 8 real-world tasks spanning 6 disciplines, measuring validation against references and deliverable quality.
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: All tasks produce validated results with benchmark deviations within engineering accuracy and complete deliverables.

research_questions.question: What architectural components are required for reliable AI-assisted engineering simulation workflows?
research_questions.hypothesis: A three-stage workflow with embedded validation gates, domain-specific agent specialization, and verified computational tools provides necessary engineering rigor.
research_questions.method: Analyze framework architecture, agent roles, information flow, and validation mechanisms.
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Clear identification of essential components with evidence from task outcomes.

research_questions.question: How does the agentic engineering approach improve traditional engineering workflows?
research_questions.hypothesis: The framework reduces time-to-first-result while maintaining engineering accuracy comparable to expert manual analysis.
research_questions.method: Assess deliverable completeness, validation metrics, and workflow characteristics across case studies.
research_questions.acceptance_criterion: Evidence of productivity improvement with maintained engineering quality.

paper_type_details: Type 4 Application paper - engineering case study demonstrating novel methodology
